channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: In a time crunch? 3 ways GenAI can come through in a clutch
item.description: In just a couple of years, generative AI (GenAI) has made a big impact on the way people, companies, and entire industries think about work. It’s helping doctors and nurses, who spend more than a third of their work week on paperwork, free up more time to focus on patients.
item.dc:creator: AJ Shankar, Fast Company

item.title: China’s DeepSeek web version is raising security alarms. Here’s why
item.description: The website of the Chinese artificial intelligence company DeepSeek, whose chatbot became the most downloaded app in the United States, has computer code that could send some user login information to a Chinese state-owned telecommunications company that has been barred from operating in the United
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: DOGE team seeks access to other agency systems after getting Treasury data
item.description: A Treasury official says DOGE team members have “read-only” access to the data from its payment systems as part of an “operational efficiency assessment." The post DOGE team seeks access to other agency systems after getting Treasury data first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Want to rent a $300,000 AMD MI300A supercomputer for free for seven days? Gigabyte wants to hear from you ASAP
item.description: Apply through Gigabyte Launchpad, where your project will be reviewed for innovation and commercial value.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Elon Musk’s China ties make a bad situation worse
item.description: In the first weeks of Donald Trump’s second term in office, Elon Musk has been busy seizing control of instruments of government power, causing many to wonder who is really in charge. “Elon Musk is President,” The Atlantic’s Jonathan Lemire declared. “There has never been a private citizen like him.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: Kyndryl expands Palo Alto deal to offer managed SASE service
item.description: Kyndryl has expanded its alliance with Palo Alto Networks to add secure access service edge (SASE) services to its managed services offerings.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: LinkedIn is releasing even more video tools as it looks to take on TikTok, Instagram
item.description: LinkedIn is making it easier for users to engage with videos, and easier for creators to see the results.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Panasonic may leave the TV business, and that would be a crying shame
item.description: Japanese electronics manufacturer Panasonic is considering a sale of its TV business, only a year after it re-entered the US market.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The Google Pixel 9a freebies have seemingly leaked, but there’s one notable omission
item.description: The Google Pixel 9a might come with a trio of free subscriptions, but could miss out on an AI upgrade.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple lashes out at iPhone porn app maker and the EU rules allowing its download
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple has chided a newly introduced pornography app available in the European Union, warning that the bloc’s digital rules opening the way for third-party app store downloads undermines consumer confidence in the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Así es el piloto que compara mediante IA generativa ofertas de proveedores en licitaciones
item.description: Carreras Grupo Logístico e IBM, en colaboración con Seidor, han desarrollado un proyecto piloto para comparar automáticamente ofertas de proveedores en licitaciones mediante el uso de inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The value of Trump’s memecoin has dropped more than 75% since inauguration
item.description: Donald Trump drew plenty of criticism by launching his own branded memecoin three days before his inauguration, including from the crypto community which argued he was making a mockery of the crypto world by tying himself to the memecoin world, which could damage efforts to make crypto reforms.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Robinhood halts Super Bowl betting contracts after CFTC request
item.description: Robinhood said on Tuesday it is rolling back the event contracts that would let users bet on the result of the Super Bowl clash this weekend, after the online brokerage received a request from the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC).
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: AWS tightens default security on Redshift
item.description: Amazon’s security improvements for its AWS Redshift managed data warehouse service are welcome additions, says an expert.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Judge says Elon Musk's claims of harm from OpenAI are a 'stretch' but welcomes possible trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Elon Musk's lawyers faced off with OpenAI in court Tuesday as a federal judge weighed the billionaire's request for a court order that would block the ChatGPT maker from converting itself to a for-profit company. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Google's rising holiday's season ad sales aren't enough to ease worries about AI letdown
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google's digital ad sales continued to grow at a healthy clip during the holiday season, but that wasn't enough to offset investors' worries about whether its big bet on artificial intelligence will be lucrative as once envisioned.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tech layoffs this year: A timeline
item.description: 2025 began in turmoil, with layoffs at some of the largest tech companies despite the support shown by the new US administration. 2024 had been a year of recovery, with the pace of layoffs slowing and IT employment the highest for years following two years of massive IT layoff in 2022 and 2023.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Quantum networking advances on Earth and in space
item.description: Cisco’s Quantum Summit brought together quantum computing experts last week from around academia, government, and industry and highlighted the progress we’ve seen over the past few months.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Mystery OPM server that aided buyout offers prompts House Democrat probe
item.description: House Oversight Democrats are demanding answers about the installation of a “server of unknown nature and origin” at the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) that aided the agency in sending buyout offers to federal employees.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: How to easily transfer files and photos off a computer to your hard drive
item.description: It's not just about freeing up space on your computer, Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says. It's about protecting your digital memories and important documents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Public cloud providers are missing the mark with AI
item.description: Microsoft’s recent earnings call painted a sobering picture. Despite the company’s aggressive push into artificial intelligence and substantial investments in infrastructure, growth numbers fell short of expectations.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google makes its appeal to overturn jury verdict branding the Play Store as illegal monopoly
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google went to appeals court Monday in an attempt to convince a three-judge panel to overturn a jury's verdict declaring its app store for Android smartphones as an illegal monopoly and block the penalties imposed by a federal judge to stop the misbehavior.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bill Gates shares his thoughts on vaccine backlash, Intel's woes and Google's antitrust battle
item.description: Although Microsoft founder Bill Gates sat down for an interview with The Associated Press to discuss his new memoir, “Source Code: My Beginnings,” he also shared his views on a variety of other topics, including vaccine conspiracy views about him, his thoughts on the struggles of longtime computer c
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What is NaaS? Providers, delivery models, and benefits explained
item.description: Network as a service (NaaS) is a cloud service model that’s designed to let enterprise IT professionals order network infrastructure components from a menu of options, have them configured to fit their business needs, and have the whole thing delivered, running and managed in a matter of hours inste
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: US cybersecurity agency's future role in elections remains murky under the Trump administration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nation’s cybersecurity agency has played a critical role in helping states shore up the defenses of their voting systems, but its election mission appears uncertain amid sustained criticism from Republicans and key figures in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Industry Exchange Cyber 2025: Mattermost’s Bill Anderson, Matthew Heideman on how DoD benefits from platform approach to collaboration
item.description: Mattermost’s tech experts explain how they create the “connective tissues of information, people and data” for this secure collaboration platform.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network
